URL: https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies

SUMMARY:
Anyone can grow food, and these Freight Farmers are proof!  Within just a few months of launching their Freight Farm and opening a new grocery store, the Mountain Fresh Produce team has consistently sold out 100% of their weekly produce, helping to eliminate food waste and drastically reduce high food miles.

# **Case Studies**

![]()

## Customer success stories from our Freight Farmers worldwide

### Our farmers come from all over the world, with different backgrounds, levels of experience, and missions. One thing they have in common? They care about serving their communities fresh and local food.

### [Schools →](#case-studies-schools)

[Schools →](#case-studies-schools)

### [Nonprofits & Institutions →](#case-studies-nonprofits)

[Nonprofits & Institutions →](#case-studies-nonprofits)

### [Small Business →](#case-studies-business)

[Small Business →](#case-studies-business)

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/54ec7e4fe4b0866fef062b8b/b1f457a8-0962-4b41-a27d-78c7a5fd8209/Freight+Farmer+Gourmet+Caterers+Case+Study+Spotlight.jpg)

See how Gourmet Caterers elevated their menu with an on-site container farm, delivering fresh, flavorful ingredients every week.

[**Gourmet Caterers Case Study** →](/case-studies/gourmet-caterers)

## Small Businesses

### Anyone can grow food, and these Freight Farmers are proof!

![Beats Per Minute Farms]()

**Kansas City, Missouri**

A veteran-owned business based in Kansas City, has revolutionized the local grocery market with its hydroponically-grown basil, using Freight Farms’ technology to supply over 50 stores.

[Read Case Study](/case-studies/beats-per-minute)

![Ferncreek Farms]()

**Orlando, Florida**

Confronted with high demand and Florida’s harsh growing conditions, Jenny Austin implemented a Freight Farm to deliver premium culinary herbs to the top restaurants in her area.

[Read Case Study](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/ferncreek-farms)

![Mountain Fresh Produce]()

**Pinedale, Wyoming**

Within just a few months of launching their Freight Farm and opening a new grocery store, the Mountain Fresh Produce team has consistently sold out 100% of their weekly produce, helping to eliminate food waste and drastically reduce high food miles.

[Read Case Study](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/mountain-fresh-produce)

![Ditto Foods]()

**Chicago, Illinois**

Urban farmers Derek Drake and Brad Schiever are striving to build up their community through fresh food access while creating a thriving, profitable business.

[Read Case Study](/case-studies/ditto-foods)

![Fare House Farms]()

**The Woodlands, Texas**

Alex discovered a passion for farming, farmers markets, and supporting her Texas community.

[Read Case Study](/fare-house-farms)

![Harvest]()

**Umeå, Sweden**

Daniel, Tobias, and Ellen grow fresh and sustainable food year-round in Northern Sweden.

[Read Case Study](/harvest-sweden)

![BrightBox Farms]()

**Kodiak, Alaska**

Siene and Gideon brought container farming to their remote Alaskan island community.

[Read Case Study](/bright-box-farms)

## Schools

### Schools and universities use Freight Farms to bring food production to campuses and provide authentic learning opportunities.

![Methacton High School]()

**Eagleville, Pennsylvania**  
  
Methacton High School has integrated a Freight Farm into its science curriculum to give students real-world experience with hydroponic farming, sustainability, and business operations.  
  
[Read Case Study](/methacton-highschool-case-study)

![Floyd County School of Innovation]()

**Martin, Kentucky**

FCSI built its FFA program around a Freight Farm—empowering students with hands-on STEM and career training. Launched in 2020 with just 80 students, the school now serves 200 through its unique opt-in, part-time model.

[Read Case Study](/case-studies/floyd-county-school-innovation-ffa)

![University of North Texas]()

**Denton, Texas**

The University of North Texas Dining Services has earned a stellar reputation nationwide for its unwavering commitment to sustainability and self-sufficiency.

[Read Case Study](/case-studies/university-of-north-texas)

![Morgan Hill Unified School District]()

**Morgan Hill, California**

MHUSD grows lettuce in their Freight Farm to feed 8,400 students and raises the bar for farm-to-school programs.

[Read Case Study](/case-studies/morgan-hill-unified-school-district)

![Auburn University]()

**Auburn, Alabama**

The trailblazing sustainable college campus implements two Greenery™ farms to provide lettuce for campus dining, with students in the horticulture program doing the work of growing the produce in the vertical farms, gaining valuable hands-on learning opportunities in the process.

[Read Case Study](/case-studies/auburn-university)

## Nonprofits & Institutions

### Nonprofits and institutions serve their missions through growing sustainable, fresh goodness and offering farm-based programming.

![Connecticut Foodshare]()

**Wallingford, Connecticut**

[**Connecticut Foodshare**](https://www.ctfoodshare.org/), the state’s largest food bank, has transformed its ability to provide fresh, nutritious produce year-round through hydroponic farming with Freight Farms.

[Read Case Study](/case-studies/ct-foodshare)

![AdventHealth Celebration]()

**Celebration, Florida**

AdventHealth Celebration, the acute care facility that delivers medical services to Walt Disney World and Greater Orlando, implemented an on-site Freight Farm to provide local, nutrient-dense produce to its staff and patients every day.

[Read Case Study](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/adventhealth-celebration)

![Regional Food Bank of Northeastern New York]()

**Latham, New York**

This food bank has tackled food insecurity with the addition of an on-site Freight Farm, providing fresh, culturally relevant produce to communities in need while reducing reliance on inconsistent donations.

[Read Case Study](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/regional-food-bank-neny)

![Boys & Girls Clubs of Metro South]()

**Brockton and Taunton, Massachusetts**

Kids learn to grow, try new foods, and participate in educational farming activities. All the while, they're learning STEM concepts and SEL skills for brighter, healthier futures.

[Read Case Study](/case-studies/boys-and-girls-clubs-metro-south)

![Seven Stars Resort and Spa]()

**Turks and Caicos**

This five-star luxury resort has transformed its approach to sourcing fresh produce, achieving significant cost savings and addressing environmental concerns.

[Read Case Study](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/seven-stars-resort)

![Broadview Federal Credit Union]()

**Albany, New York**

Learn how this Federal Credit Union grows and donates over 10,000 lbs of produce a year with 4 container farms.

[Read Case Study](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/broadview-federal-credit-union)

![Lotus House]()

**Miami, Florida**

This nonprofit uses fresh food in the fight to end homelessness and combat food insecurity.

[Read Case Study](/lotus-house-miami)

![ICA Maxi]()

**Högskolan, Sweden**

This Nordic grocery store grows lettuce onsite to create year-round local food options for shoppers.

[Read Case Study](/ica-maxi)

SUMMARY:
Freight Farms, a Boston-based container farming company, has been at the forefront of the container farming revolution. Their flagship product,

FULL CONTENT:
Where there is a plant factory, there is Opticlimatefarm.

![OPTICLIMATE]()

# Success Stories of Container Farming Companies: Driving the Future of Agriculture

**The Future of Agriculture: Success Stories of Container Farming Companies**

  

As the world population continues to grow at an alarming rate, the pressure to produce more food in a sustainable and efficient manner has become a top priority. Traditional farming methods are facing numerous challenges such as climate change, water scarcity, and limited arable land. In this ever-changing landscape, container farming has emerged as a promising solution to feed the world's population while mitigating the challenges faced by traditional agriculture. In this article, we will explore the success stories of container farming companies that are driving the future of agriculture.

  

**Revolutionizing Farming with Shipping Containers**

  

Shipping containers, once solely known for transporting goods, are being repurposed to revolutionize the way we grow our food. Container farming involves converting these durable steel boxes into self-contained farming units that can be set up virtually anywhere, from urban rooftops to barren deserts. These modified containers are equipped with environmental controls, including lighting, irrigation, and climate control systems, enabling farmers to cultivate crops year-round, regardless of external weather conditions.

  

**The Rise of Vertical Farming**

  

One of the key advantages of container farming is its ability to embrace vertical farming. With limited space available, especially in urban areas, vertical farming maximizes productivity by growing crops in stacked layers within the container. This innovation not only utilizes space more efficiently but also reduces the need for extensive land use. Vertical farming promotes a high yield per square foot, allowing farmers to cultivate a wide variety of crops while minimizing environmental impact.

  

Vertical farming also eliminates the need for harmful pesticides and herbicides, as the controlled environment prevents pest infestations and diseases. By adopting this technique, container farming companies are leading the way in sustainable, chemical-free agriculture.

  

**Case Study: Freight Farms**

  

Freight Farms, a Boston-based container farming company, has been at the forefront of the container farming revolution. Their flagship product, the Leafy Green Machine (LGM), is a fully equipped hydroponic farm housed within a 40-foot shipping container. The LGM boasts features such as LED lighting, a nutrient delivery system, and automated climate controls, providing optimal growing conditions for crops.

  

Through their innovative technology and business model, Freight Farms has paved the way for local food production. Their container farms are empowering communities to grow fresh and healthy produce year-round, reducing the need for long-distance transportation and minimizing food waste. As of now, Freight Farms has installed over 200 container farms across the United States, enabling individuals and organizations to take control of their food production.

  

**Container Farming in Arid Regions**

  

Traditional agriculture faces immense challenges in arid regions where water scarcity is a significant issue. However, container farming offers a glimmer of hope in these dry landscapes. By leveraging hydroponic or aeroponic systems, which nourish plants without soil, container farms can drastically reduce water consumption compared to traditional farming methods.

  

Container farming companies like Green Sense Farms have successfully implemented their technology in arid regions such as the Middle East. Green Sense Farms operates a network of container farms in Dubai, where water resources are limited. By employing advanced water recycling and efficient irrigation systems, they are able to produce a significant amount of fresh fruits and vegetables with minimal water usage. Container farming is providing a more sustainable and resilient alternative to agriculture in water-scarce regions, ensuring food security for these vulnerable areas.

  

**Empowering Communities and Combatting Food Deserts**

  

Food deserts, areas with limited access to fresh, affordable, and nutritious food, have become a pressing issue in many urban communities. Container farming offers a practical solution to combat this problem. By bringing food production closer to the consumers, container farms eliminate the need for long transportation routes and reduce the cost associated with traditional farming methods.

  

Companies like Square Roots are leveraging container farming to address the issue of food deserts. Through their "Next-Gen Farmer Training Program," Square Roots empowers aspiring farmers to become urban agriculture entrepreneurs. Participants in the program cultivate crops in climate-controlled shipping containers, allowing them to grow fresh produce year-round. By creating a network of container farms within communities, Square Roots not only provides access to nutritious food but also helps revitalize local economies.

  

**Driving the Future of Agriculture**

  

Container farming companies are at the forefront of driving the future of agriculture. Through their innovative use of shipping containers, vertical farming techniques, and advanced technologies, they are revolutionizing the way we grow our food. These success stories highlight the immense potential of container farming to address various challenges faced by traditional agriculture, including climate change, water scarcity, and food security.

  

In conclusion, container farming is proving to be a game-changer in the agricultural industry. The success stories of companies like Freight Farms, Green Sense Farms, and Square Roots demonstrate the immense potential of container farming to transform the way we produce and consume food. With its ability to overcome limitations imposed by climate, space, and resources, container farming is paving the way for a sustainable and resilient future of agriculture.

SUMMARY:
Founded in 2012, Freight Farms debuted the first vertical hydroponic farm built inside an intermodal shipping container with the mission of

FULL CONTENT:
![Freight Farms](//images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/54ec7e4fe4b0866fef062b8b/b0cd4001-6e53-4421-9a88-22b5e80436a2/Web_Freright-Farms-Primary-Logo_350x132.png?format=1500w)
![Freight Farms](//images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/54ec7e4fe4b0866fef062b8b/b0cd4001-6e53-4421-9a88-22b5e80436a2/Web_Freright-Farms-Primary-Logo_350x132.png?format=1500w)

# Over 20 Vertical Farming Companies Commit to a Sustainable Food System

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/54ec7e4fe4b0866fef062b8b/f12ceb49-7d17-4cee-97c4-7cb082f6e005/vertical-farming-world-congress-freight-farms)

### Leading vertical farming companies have committed to a Vertical Farming Identity Statement and Industry Manifesto. An unprecedented industry momentum to contribute jointly to a sustainable transformation of global food systems.

**Signing companies are** 80 Acres, AeroFarms, Bowery, Crop One, CubicFarms, Elevate Farms, Fifth Season, Fischer Farms, Freight Farms, Infarm, Infinite Acres, Jones Food Company, Kalera, La Grangette, Ljusgårda, N.THING, Plenty, Stacked Farm, Urban Crop Solutions, Urban Harvest, Vertical Future, YesHealth Group, and ZipGrow

The Vertical Farming Manifesto was signed at the **Vertical Farming World Congress in Brussels (September 19-21)**

**Brussels, September 20, 2022 –** Leading [vertical farming](/vertical-farming) companies signed the first [Vertical Farming Manifesto](https://www.infarm.com/vertical-farming-manifesto) and [Identity](https://www.infarm.com/vertical-farming-identity-statement) — both clear and bold industry standards. They highlight the impact and contribution the industry intends to make to transform current food systems and help to solve one of humanity’s toughest challenges of providing food for a growing population in a sustainable, circular way.

The Vertical Farming Identity and the Manifesto describe the urgency of transforming current food systems and explain how vertical farming systems can contribute significantly to designing food systems which are resilient and future-proof. Through technological innovation, indoor vertical farming decouples food production from external factors like climate or public health crises. By localizing production and controlling the growing environment, vertical farming provides pesticide-free produce, uses significantly less land and water, and requires fewer transportation miles.

Toward the industry’s goal of driving the transformation of food systems, the companies strive to set high standards by aligning with globally accepted impact frameworks, for example, the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi), B Corp, or the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Furthermore, the industry is dedicated to continuously investing in research and development to improve [vertical farming](/vertical-farming) technology and grow even smarter and more sustainable food systems.

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/54ec7e4fe4b0866fef062b8b/07f6c3b1-06c0-4928-bf08-d4ead46f547d/Logo-Image_Landscape-2048x1448.jpg)

### About Freight Farms:

Founded in 2012, Freight Farms debuted the first vertical hydroponic farm built inside an intermodal shipping container with the mission of democratizing and decentralizing the local production of fresh, healthy food. Freight Farms now has the largest network of connected farms in the world, with global customers ranging from small business farmers to corporate, hospitality, retail, education, and nonprofit sectors.

#### Freight Farms Raises $17.5M Series B3 to Address Access and Sustainability in the Food System Through Container Farming

#### Freight Farms Partners with Biosphere 2 to Pioneer the Future of Indoor Agriculture

URL: https://www.igrowpreowned.com/knowledge-center/case-studies-customer-success-stories-from-our-freight-farmers-worldwide

SUMMARY:
Urban farmers Derek Drake and Brad Schiever are striving to build up their community through fresh food access while creating a thriving, profitable business.

FULL CONTENT:

# CASE STUDIES: Customer Success Stories From Our Freight Farmers Worldwide

Our farmers come from all over the world, with different backgrounds, levels of experience, and missions. One thing they have in common? They care about serving their communities fresh and local food.

### [**Schools →**](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies?utm_campaign=International%20-%2050Hz&utm_medium=email&_hsenc=p2ANqtz-_rQ0UvhRty6GiuQ-lgGlsg7hVQNj_UgOQDDNFoyLNR3wUjrEkQdaXm7S0Ylyq06K8UbWi0oMWy6tyUigRK-5PD0VMkdg&_hsmi=299615715&utm_content=299615715&utm_source=hs_automation#case-studies-schools)

### [**Nonprofits & Institutions →**](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies?utm_campaign=International%20-%2050Hz&utm_medium=email&_hsenc=p2ANqtz-_rQ0UvhRty6GiuQ-lgGlsg7hVQNj_UgOQDDNFoyLNR3wUjrEkQdaXm7S0Ylyq06K8UbWi0oMWy6tyUigRK-5PD0VMkdg&_hsmi=299615715&utm_content=299615715&utm_source=hs_automation#case-studies-nonprofits)

### [**Small Business →**](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies?utm_campaign=International%20-%2050Hz&utm_medium=email&_hsenc=p2ANqtz-_rQ0UvhRty6GiuQ-lgGlsg7hVQNj_UgOQDDNFoyLNR3wUjrEkQdaXm7S0Ylyq06K8UbWi0oMWy6tyUigRK-5PD0VMkdg&_hsmi=299615715&utm_content=299615715&utm_source=hs_automation#case-studies-business)

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/57c8695d5016e11e71be1d83/9d7d785b-c534-4e3e-b1e0-f6cc40769cc4/DSC07259%2B%281%29.jpeg)

**University of North Texas Dining Services grows produce on campus**

Read more about [**University of North Texas** →](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/university-of-north-texas)

## **Small Businesses**

### Anyone can grow food, and these Freight Farmers are proof!

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/57c8695d5016e11e71be1d83/3f5caa1d-af54-4626-86a2-308c47ac1220/_0Z65651-min.jpeg)

[**King Tide Farms**](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/king-tide-farms)

**Charleston, South Carolina**  
  
Hamilton Horne grows premium produce for Charleston restaurants. His aptitude for sales and strategies for partnering with chefs have helped him to be a highly profitable farmer.

[**Read more**](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/king-tide-farms)

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/57c8695d5016e11e71be1d83/deea4d4d-0213-43d4-823d-f997ed78a8a3/Cayman%2BCompass.jpeg)

[**Primitive Greens**](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/primitive-greens)

**George Town, Cayman Islands**

With only inconvenient shipping routes connecting the Caribbean to fresh produce, Cayman is food insecure. To alleviate the situation, Codi Whittaker, a young Freight Farmer, has become the source of fresh produce on the island.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/primitive-greens)

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/57c8695d5016e11e71be1d83/e87ce783-2ade-469f-9840-faf0c618a27e/image-asset.jpeg)

[**Ditto Foods**](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/ditto-foods)

**Chicago, Illinois**

Urban farmers Derek Drake and Brad Schiever are striving to build up their community through fresh food access while creating a thriving, profitable business.

[**Read more**](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/ditto-foods)

[Fare House Farms](https://www.freightfarms.com/fare-house-farms)

**The Woodlands, Texas**

Alex discovered a passion for farming, farmers markets, and supporting her Texas community.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/fare-house-farms)

[Harvest](https://www.freightfarms.com/harvest-sweden)

**Umeå, Sweden**

Daniel, Tobias, and Ellen grow fresh and sustainable food year-round in Northern Sweden.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/harvest-sweden)

[BrightBox Farms](https://www.freightfarms.com/bright-box-farms)

**Kodiak, Alaska**

Siene and Gideon brought container farming to their remote Alaskan island community.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/bright-box-farms)

[Town to Table](https://www.freightfarms.com/town-to-table)

**Boston, Massachusetts**

Jack turned his senior thesis into a farming business that partners with local schools and nonprofits.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/town-to-table)

[New Age Provisions](https://www.freightfarms.com/new-age-provisions)

**Indianapolis, Indiana**

Mario saw container farming as a chance to leave his 9–5 and become his own boss.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/new-age-provisions)

[Karma Farm](https://www.freightfarms.com/karma-farm)

**Monkton, Maryland**

Jon and Nat combine traditional farming with hydroponics to grow year-round and make the family farm a sustainable business.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/karma-farm)

[Clawson Greens](https://www.freightfarms.com/clawson-greens)

**Tetonia, Idaho**

Turning a 3-month growing season into a year-round business, Dave scaled from one farm to two and provides local restaurants and ski resorts with fresh greens.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/clawson-greens)

[Hammock Greens](https://www.freightfarms.com/hammock-greens)

**Miami, Florida**

Hammock Greens wanted a better way to create delicious and clean greens, without using tons of land, water, and pesticides. They built their business from one container farm to 11 — and they’re just getting started!

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/hammock-greens)

[HerbanLeaf Farms](https://www.freightfarms.com/herbanleaf-farms)

**Limassol, Cyprus**

In many ways, HerbanLeaf Farms is your typical family farm … but the family is also using their Freight Farm to make Cyprus a more independent island.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/herbanleaf-farms)

## **Schools**

### Schools and universities use Freight Farms to bring food production to campuses and provide authentic learning opportunities.

[University of North Texas](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/university-of-north-texas)

**Denton, Texas**

The University of North Texas Dining Services has earned a stellar reputation nationwide for its unwavering commitment to sustainability and self-sufficiency.

[Read More](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/university-of-north-texas)

[Floyd County School of Innovation](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/floyd-county-school-innovation-ffa)

**Martin, Kentucky**

FCSI runs an FFA program that hinges on a Freight Farm, with plant science intermixed with business, career readiness, and leadership opportunities.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/floyd-county-school-innovation-ffa)

[Morgan Hill Unified School District](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/morgan-hill-unified-school-district)

**Morgan Hill, California**

MHUSD grows lettuce in their Freight Farm to feed 8,400 students and raises the bar for farm-to-school programs.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/morgan-hill-unified-school-district)

[Mountain Vista High School](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/mountain-vista-high-school)

**Highlands Ranch, Colorado**

The campus’ Leafy Green Machine farm is utilized by teachers across disciplines to give students valuable dynamic, hands-on learning experiences.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/mountain-vista-high-school)

[Auburn University](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/auburn-university)

**Auburn, Alabama**

Horticulture students grow food for all students to enjoy at the campus dining hall.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/auburn-university)

[Saint Joseph's College of Maine](https://www.freightfarms.com/saint-josephs-college)

**Standish, Maine**

SCJME built a curriculum and work-study program around a Freight Farm to inspire student farmers.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/saint-josephs-college)

[Clark University & Sodexo](https://www.freightfarms.com/clark-university)

**Worcester, Massachusetts**

Clark grows food just steps away from their campus dining hall to bring students the freshest greens possible.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/clark-university)

[Maryville University](https://www.freightfarms.com/maryville-university)

**St. Louis, Missouri**

The Freight Farm is an important part of a larger sustainability initiative on Maryville’s campus, where students have the opportunity to intern on the farm and eat the fresh produce.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/maryville-university)

## **Nonprofits & Institutions**

### Nonprofits and institutions serve their missions through growing sustainable, fresh goodness and offering farm-based programming.

[Boys & Girls Clubs of Metro South](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/boys-and-girls-clubs-metro-south)

**Brockton and Taunton, Massachusetts**

Kids learn to grow, try new foods, and participate in educational farming activities. All the while, they're learning STEM concepts and SEL skills for brighter, healthier futures.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/boys-and-girls-clubs-metro-south)

[San Antonio Clubhouse](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/san-antonio-clubhouse)

**San Antonio, Texas**

Freight Farming is an opportunity for members of San Antonio Clubhouse to be empowered and overcome mental health challenges.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/case-studies/san-antonio-clubhouse)

[Lotus House](https://www.freightfarms.com/lotus-house-miami)

**Miami, Florida**

This nonprofit uses fresh food in the fight to end homelessness and combat food insecurity.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/lotus-house-miami)

[Pillsbury United Communities](https://www.freightfarms.com/pillsbury-united-communities)

**Minneapolis, Minnesota**

PUC runs two food shelves and two daily hot meals programs, which get their fresh foods from the organization’s four urban gardens and their Freight Farm.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/pillsbury-united-communities)

[ICA Maxi](https://www.freightfarms.com/ica-maxi)

**Högskolan, Sweden**

This Nordic grocery store grows lettuce onsite to create year-round local food options for shoppers.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/ica-maxi)

[Zeponic Farms](https://www.freightfarms.com/blog/farmer-qa-zeponic-farms)

**Springfield, Virginia**

Zach Zepf launched this farm to offer employment and educational experiences to individuals with special needs.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/blog/farmer-qa-zeponic-farms)

[SEFCU & Boys and Girls Club](https://www.freightfarms.com/blog/csr-sefcu-boys-and-girls-club)

**Troy, New York**

SEFCU expanded their corporate philanthropy by empowering their local Boys & Girls Club community to start container farming.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/blog/csr-sefcu-boys-and-girls-club)

[OD Greens](https://www.freightfarms.com/blog/farmer-qa-od-greens)

**Cleveland, Ohio**

Don started OD Greens to help veterans re-assimilate and develop job skills through farming.

[Read more](https://www.freightfarms.com/blog/farmer-qa-od-greens)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Freight Farms revolutionizes local food production with their climate-controlled shipping containers, enabling year-round hydroponic farming and reducing food miles significantly.
Spread Co. utilizes vertical farming techniques to maximize production efficiency, providing fresh greens to urban areas while minimizing environmental impact through resource-efficient practices.
AeroFarms leads in indoor vertical farming, employing aeroponic technology to grow nutrient-rich crops without soil, ensuring sustainable, pesticide-free produce for local communities.
Vertical farming initiatives enhance food security, create jobs, and foster community engagement by providing fresh produce directly to consumers, reducing reliance on distant food sources.
